key_insight: Sonochemical treatment of Janus TMD monolayers on Au foils creates strain and defects that substantially boost hydrogen evolution activity, bringing performance close to Pt for SeMoS.
engineering_relevance: This suggests a practical route to tune 2D electrocatalysts for low-overpotential HER using scalable post-processing in polar solvents.
method: Grow Janus TMD monolayers (SeMoS, SeWS) on Au foils by chemical vapor deposition.
Apply sonochemical treatment in polar and non-polar solvents.
Compare HER performance against parent TMDs and support mechanisms with DFT plus experimental analysis.
evidence_signal: Water-treated SeMoS reached ~63 mV overpotential, ~42 mV/dec Tafel slope, and ~10^-3 mA cm^-2 exchange current density, indicating near-platinum-like activity.
watch_item: The enhancement depends on substrate/solvent-driven restructuring and defect formation, so durability and reproducibility under operating conditions need careful validation.
paper_title: Sonochemically Boosted Hydrogen Evolution Activity of Janus TMD Monolayers
